weeks.label: Week 1
weeks.title: Foundations
weeks.subtitle: How AI works + your first prompts

days.title: What is AI & how do LLMs work?
days.description: Start with the most important question: how does this technology actually work? You don't need to understand the math — just the intuition.

course.name: How Large Language Models Work
course.description: The best visual explanation of how neural networks and LLMs predict text. No math required — pure intuition.

tasks: Watch the video once fully, then re-watch the key sections
Write 5 sentences explaining AI to a friend in your own words
Open Claude or ChatGPT and say hello — try your first prompt
days.tip: Don't overthink the tech. Focus on the concept of 'predicting next words based on patterns.'

days.title: Your first prompts
days.description: Now that you know how AI works, it's time to actually use it. Write as many prompts as possible today — quality comes from quantity at this stage.

course.name: Introduction to Generative AI
course.description: Google's beginner-friendly intro covering what generative AI is, how it works, and its real-world applications.

tasks: Complete Google's Intro to Generative AI course
Write 10 different prompts on topics you care about
Note which responses felt useful vs. vague — save your best 3
days.tip: The quality of your output is directly tied to the clarity of your input.

days.title: Prompt anatomy — role, context, task
days.description: The single framework that will improve every prompt you write: every strong prompt has three parts. Learn this once and use it forever.

course.name: Prompt Engineering for ChatGPT
course.description: Dr. Jules White's university course. Modules 1 and 2 cover prompt patterns and the core structure framework.

tasks: Complete Modules 1 & 2 of the Vanderbilt course
Rewrite 3 of your Day 2 prompts using Role + Context + Task
Compare old vs. new outputs — document what changed
days.tip: Example: 'You are a marketing expert [role]. I run a small bakery [context]. Write 5 Instagram captions [task].'

days.title: Zero-shot vs few-shot prompting
days.description: Two of the most important prompting techniques. Zero-shot = no examples. Few-shot = you show AI what you want first.

course.name: ChatGPT Prompt Engineering for Developers
course.description: Taught by Andrew Ng & Isa Fulford from OpenAI. Covers zero-shot, few-shot, and core prompting best practices.

tasks: Complete the full DeepLearning.AI short course
Write one zero-shot and one few-shot prompt for the same task
Document which gave better results and why
days.tip: Few-shot prompting often dramatically improves output quality for creative or structured tasks.

days.title: Output formats & structure
days.description: AI can respond in almost any format — but only if you ask. Today you'll master output control: tables, bullets, JSON, markdown, and length constraints.

course.name: Generative AI: Prompt Engineering Basics
course.description: IBM's 4.8-star rated course (351k+ learners). Covers prompt techniques, output formatting, and structured response best practices.

tasks: Complete the IBM course section on output formats
Ask AI to respond in bullets, tables, JSON, and markdown for the same prompt
Practice specifying length: '200 words max', '5 bullet points'
days.tip: AI can mimic almost any format — you just have to ask explicitly.

days.title: Iterate & refine prompts
days.description: Prompting is a conversation, not a one-shot request. Today you learn the art of iteration — how to go from a good prompt to a great one in 3–5 follow-ups.

course.name: Anthropic's Prompt Engineering Guide
course.description: Anthropic's official guide on writing better prompts for Claude. Covers iteration, clarity, and the most common mistakes.

tasks: Read the full Anthropic prompt engineering guide
Pick one topic and write 5 different prompt variations
Build your first prompt bank with 10+ entries in a doc
days.tip: Prompting is a conversation. Iteration is the skill — not writing the perfect prompt on the first try.

days.title: Week 1 review & reflection
days.description: Don't skip this day. Reflection is what turns practice into skill. Review everything you've learned and set yourself up for Week 2.

course.name: AI For Everyone — Week 1
course.description: Andrew Ng's non-technical AI course for everyone. Week 1 solidifies your conceptual foundation.

tasks: Complete Week 1 of AI For Everyone by Andrew Ng
Review all prompts from this week — pick your top 3
Write your goal for Week 2 and put it somewhere visible
days.tip: Reflection is what turns practice into real skill. The 20 minutes you spend here will accelerate everything that follows.

days.title: Chain-of-thought prompting
days.description: Five words that dramatically improve AI reasoning: 'Let's think step by step.' Chain-of-thought is one of the most research-backed techniques available.

course.name: Prompt Engineering Guide — CoT Chapter
course.description: The most cited free resource on chain-of-thought prompting. Clear examples and research-backed techniques.

tasks: Read the full CoT chapter at PromptingGuide.ai
Try 'Think step by step' on 5 different problems or tasks
Compare CoT vs. standard responses — what specifically changed?
days.tip: 'Let's think step by step' is one of the most powerful 5-word prompts ever discovered in AI research.

days.title: Persona & role prompting
days.description: Assigning an expert role to AI changes everything: the vocabulary, depth, tone, and perspective of the response shifts dramatically.

course.name: Prompt Engineering for ChatGPT — Module 3
course.description: Covers the Persona Pattern and expert role assignment with real examples and exercises.

tasks: Complete Module 3 of the Vanderbilt course
Try 5 different expert personas on the same task
Document how tone, vocabulary, and depth change with each persona
days.tip: Persona prompting is like having access to a specialist for every task, for free, instantly.

days.title: Negative prompting & constraints
days.description: Telling AI what NOT to do is just as powerful as telling it what to do. Constraints improve quality and force more creative thinking.

course.name: Advanced Prompt Techniques
course.description: Covers negative prompting, output constraints, and how to refine prompts to eliminate unwanted patterns.

tasks: Read the techniques section at PromptingGuide.ai
Write 5 prompts combining positive AND negative instructions
Try: 'No clichés', 'Don't use jargon', 'Avoid bullet points'
days.tip: Constraints often improve creativity. Limits force better solutions.

days.title: System prompts & custom instructions
days.description: System prompts define the AI's 'character' for an entire session. They're the most powerful tool for building consistent, reliable AI assistants.

course.name: Building Systems with the ChatGPT API
course.description: Covers system prompts, multi-turn conversations, and building AI-powered systems. Coding parts are optional.

tasks: Complete the DeepLearning.AI systems short course
Write a custom system prompt for your personal AI assistant
Test it with 10 different user messages — refine based on results
days.tip: System prompts define the character of the AI for an entire session. Master this and you've built your own assistant.

days.title: Prompt templates & reusability
days.description: Templates are your productivity multiplier. Build a prompt once, use it for every similar task forever.

course.name: ChatGPT & AI Prompt Templates
course.description: Practical course on building reusable prompt templates. Search for free coupon codes on CouponScorpion.com before purchasing.

tasks: Build 3 reusable prompt templates using [TOPIC], [TONE], [AUDIENCE] variables
Test each template with 3 different inputs to verify it works broadly
Add all templates to your prompt bank with usage notes
days.tip: Templates are your competitive advantage. One hour building them saves hundreds of hours later.

days.title: Handling AI mistakes & hallucinations
days.description: AI is confidently wrong sometimes. Understanding why this happens — and how to prompt around it — is a critical professional skill.

course.name: Why AI Hallucinates — MIT Explainer
course.description: MIT's explainer on generative AI reliability, hallucinations, and the fundamental reasons LLMs make things up.

tasks: Read the MIT article on AI hallucinations
Practice prompting AI to signal uncertainty: 'If unsure, say so'
Add reliability checks to 5 of your most-used prompts
days.tip: Always verify important facts. AI is confident even when wrong. Your judgment is the quality filter.

days.title: Week 2 project — build a prompt library
days.description: Your Week 2 deliverable: a structured, organized prompt library you'll use for the rest of the 30 days and beyond.

course.name: Prompt Library in Notion — Tutorial
course.description: Search 'prompt library Notion template' on YouTube. Multiple free tutorials walk through setting up organized prompt databases.

tasks: Create a structured prompt library in Notion or Google Docs
Organize by category: Writing, Research, Marketing, Coding, Analysis
Add at least 20 prompts with notes on when and how to use each
days.tip: Your prompt library is your most valuable AI asset. It compounds in value every day you add to it.

days.title: AI for blog writing
days.description: AI as a co-writer — not a ghostwriter. Today you learn to use AI to draft faster while keeping your authentic voice in the final piece.

course.name: AI-Powered Writing & Content
course.description: HubSpot's free content marketing course with an AI writing module covering blog creation, SEO, and editing AI drafts.

tasks: Complete the AI writing module in HubSpot Academy
Write a full blog post outline with AI assistance
Draft one complete section using AI, then rewrite it in your own voice
days.tip: AI is a co-writer, not a ghostwriter. Your edits, perspective, and experience make content valuable.

days.title: Social media content at scale
days.description: The biggest unlock in social media: create 30 days of content in one session. Today you build a replicable system for high-volume content creation.

course.name: Social Media Marketing with AI
course.description: Meta's free social media content strategy course. Combine with AI tools to create content at scale across platforms.

tasks: Complete the Meta Blueprint social strategy module
Write 30 social posts on one topic using AI
Create a 2-week content calendar for one platform
days.tip: Batch content creation with AI. Produce a full month of posts in an hour.

days.title: Email marketing copy
days.description: Email is still the highest-ROI content format. Today you master AI-assisted email copywriting: subject lines, body copy, sequences, and CTAs.

course.name: Email Marketing Certification — Module 5
course.description: Module 5 on copywriting covers email structure, subject line psychology, and CTAs — then apply AI to execute each.

tasks: Complete Module 5 of HubSpot's email marketing course
Write a cold outreach email and a 3-email welcome sequence using AI
Generate 5 subject line variations for each email and compare them
days.tip: Test subject lines with AI before sending. Ask it to predict open rates and explain why.

days.title: Video scripts & YouTube content
days.description: Video is the highest-reach content format. AI dramatically speeds up scriptwriting, structuring hooks, and generating title ideas.

course.name: YouTube Creator Academy — Scripting
course.description: YouTube's official creator training on scripting, hooks, and storytelling structure. Use AI to execute what you learn here.

tasks: Study the YouTube Creator Academy scripting module
Write a full 3-minute video script on a topic you know — AI-assisted
Generate 10 title ideas and 5 thumbnail text options with AI
days.tip: The hook — your first 15 seconds — matters most. Spend the most prompt iterations here.

days.title: SEO content with AI
days.description: SEO + AI is one of the most valuable skill combinations right now. Today you produce search-optimized content at scale without sacrificing quality.

course.name: SEO Certification — Content Module
course.description: Learn SEO fundamentals, then use AI to execute: keyword research, content briefs, meta descriptions, and FAQ sections.

tasks: Complete the HubSpot SEO and content module
Write a 500-word SEO-optimized article section with AI assistance
Use AI to generate meta descriptions, H2 headings, and FAQ answers
days.tip: Combine AI speed with human SEO judgment. Never publish without reviewing for accuracy and originality.

days.title: AI images & visual content
days.description: Words aren't the only content AI can create. Today you explore AI image generation: how to write prompts that produce professional-quality visuals.

course.name: Midjourney & AI Image — Beginner Guide
course.description: Search 'Midjourney beginner guide 2025' or 'DALL-E 3 tutorial' on YouTube. Many excellent free walkthroughs available.

tasks: Watch one complete beginner tutorial on an AI image generation tool
Write your first 10 image prompts: style + subject + mood + lighting
Generate images and compare — which prompts produced the best results?
days.tip: Image prompts work best with specific adjectives: 'photorealistic', 'flat illustration', 'cinematic lighting'.

days.title: Week 3 project — full content package
days.description: Your Week 3 deliverable: a complete content package for one topic or product. This becomes your portfolio piece.

course.name: Content Strategy for Professionals — Week 1
course.description: Northwestern's strategic content course. Audit Week 1 to learn how to think about content packages holistically.

tasks: Complete Week 1 of Northwestern's content strategy course
Create your full package: 1 blog, 5 social posts, 1 email, 3 images
Review everything and add your authentic voice to every piece
days.tip: This project is your portfolio piece. Focus on quality over speed today.

days.title: Build an AI workflow
days.description: Individual prompts are powerful. AI workflows are transformative. Today you map and build a repeatable system that automates a real weekly task.

course.name: AI Automation & Workflow Design
course.description: Make (formerly Integromat) offers free courses on automating workflows with AI. Learn to chain tools and automate repeatable tasks.

tasks: Complete the Make.com intro to automation course
Map one real weekly task that could be AI-assisted
Build and test a step-by-step AI workflow for that task — time yourself
days.tip: The goal isn't to replace your work. It's to spend more time on the parts only you can do.

days.title: AI for research & summarization
days.description: AI can read, compare, and summarize sources faster than any human. Today you develop a repeatable research process using AI as your assistant.

course.name: Perplexity AI Research Tutorial
course.description: Search 'Perplexity AI tutorial 2025' on YouTube. Learn to use AI for deep research, source comparison, and structured summarization.

tasks: Learn Perplexity AI via a YouTube tutorial
Paste a long article into Claude and practice extracting key takeaways
Use AI to compare 3 different sources on one topic you care about
days.tip: AI can read faster than you. Use it as a research assistant, not a replacement for your own judgment.

days.title: Prompt chaining & multi-step tasks
days.description: The output of one prompt becomes the input of the next. Prompt chains simulate a creative team working in sequence.

course.name: LangChain for LLM Application Development
course.description: Andrew Ng's course on chaining AI prompts into multi-step workflows. Focus on the concepts — the code is optional.

tasks: Complete the LangChain short course (concepts first, code optional)
Design a 5-step prompt chain: idea → expand → write → critique → revise
Test your chain on a real project and document what each step produces
days.tip: Prompt chains simulate a creative team working together. Each step builds on the last.

days.title: AI tools ecosystem deep dive
days.description: There are dozens of AI tools available. Today you map the landscape, test the key ones, and build your personal toolkit.

course.name: Generative AI Tools Overview
course.description: LinkedIn Learning's overview of the AI tools landscape. Free with a 1-month trial. Covers Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini, Jasper, Perplexity and more.

tasks: Complete the LinkedIn Learning AI tools overview (free trial)
Run the same prompt in Claude, ChatGPT, and Gemini — compare results
Choose your 2–3 primary tools based on what fits your workflow best
days.tip: Different AI tools have different strengths. Build a toolkit, not a single dependency.

days.title: Ethical AI & responsible use
days.description: Being a responsible AI user is a professional skill. Understanding bias, hallucinations, copyright, and appropriate use builds trust.

course.name: Responsible AI Practices
course.description: Google's official responsible AI course covering bias, fairness, transparency, and ethical use in real-world applications.

tasks: Complete Google's Responsible AI course
Write your personal AI use policy — one page, honest and specific
List 5 tasks where you will use AI and 5 where you won't
days.tip: Being a responsible AI user is a professional skill. It builds trust in every relationship where AI is involved.

days.title: Teach what you know
days.description: Teaching is the fastest path to mastery. If you can explain a concept clearly to someone else, you truly understand it.

course.name: The Feynman Technique
course.description: Search 'Feynman Technique explained' on YouTube. Learn to teach concepts simply — the fastest known method for solidifying your knowledge.

tasks: Write a short guide: '5 prompts every [your profession] should know'
Explain one AI concept to a friend or colleague today
Post one AI tip or insight on LinkedIn, X, or in a community
days.tip: Teaching is the fastest path to mastery. If you can explain it simply, you own it.

days.title: Final project — showcase piece
days.description: Your 30-day finale: build something real. This is your proof of skill — more valuable than any certificate.

course.name: Portfolio Building with Google Career Resources
course.description: Google's free career development resources on building an AI skills portfolio and presenting your work professionally.

tasks: Build a real deliverable: newsletter, content strategy, landing page, or tool
Use at least 5 prompting techniques from the past 4 weeks
Document your process — what AI helped with vs. what you added
days.tip: Real output beats any certificate. This project is your portfolio. Make it count.

days.title: Review & gap analysis
days.description: 30 days is a beginning, not an end. Today you honestly assess where you've grown, where the gaps are, and chart your next steps.

course.name: Advanced Prompt Engineering — What's Next
course.description: Search 'advanced prompt engineering 2025' on Medium or Towards Data Science to find articles pointing to your next learning frontier.

tasks: Review your prompt library — what categories are still thin?
Identify 3 specific areas where you still feel uncertain
Find one course, community, or book to go deeper into your weakest area
days.tip: 30 days is a beginning, not an end. Identify your next level now while the momentum is alive.

days.title: Celebrate & plan what's next
days.description: You did it. 30 days of consistent, deliberate AI learning. Take a moment to acknowledge that — then set your next target.

course.name: AI Power User — Andrew Ng's Newest Course
course.description: Andrew Ng's newest course on becoming an AI power user in your daily work. The perfect graduation course for Day 30.

tasks: Complete Andrew Ng's AI Power User course as your 30-day graduation
Write a reflection: what changed in 30 days? What surprised you?
Set your 60-day AI goal and share one win or insight publicly
days.tip: You started. You showed up. That's what most people never do. Now keep building.
